sourceMovie.title: The Amateur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Amateur (2025) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James Hawes's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Amateur clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Amateur?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, Salt, Spy Game—each shares DNA with The Amateur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Amateur?
faq.answer: The Amateur (2025) is best described as Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Amateur worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
